steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: On the left: the independent clause “detected a phenomenon occurring in Coma Berenices, 4.7 billion light-years from Earth” is complete but needs what follows. On the right: “the explosion of a massive star, also known as a supernova, an extremely bright yet transient event that typically results from the collapse of a star's core” is a noun phrase serving as an appositive that explains what the phenomenon is.

steps.title: Apply the rule for connecting an independent clause with an appositive
steps.content: A colon introduces an explanation or appositive. Choice D (Earth: the) correctly marks where this explanation of the phenomenon begins. A semicolon (A) requires two independent clauses, not present; a period (C) would awkwardly separate the phenomenon from its explanation; option B adds “it was,” introducing a new clause instead. That matches choice D — D is correct.
